key_insight: A single "cliff token" can trigger a model’s transition from correct mathematical reasoning to failure, and removing that token often restores successful recovery.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical debugging and training target for LLM reasoning systems: identify high-risk tokens, intervene at the exact failure trigger, and improve robustness with token-level optimization.
method: Detect cliff tokens using an adaptive threshold on token-wise potential drops with a one-sided two-proportion z-test.
Classify cliffs into deterministic, uncertain, and sampled-off types using greedy choice and token entropy.
Apply single-token preference optimization at cliff positions (Cliff-DPO) to improve reasoning performance.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluate seven models on GSM1K, MATH500, and AIME 2025, reporting that deleting the first cliff token and resampling recovers pass@64 to 1.0, while Cliff-DPO improves accuracy by up to +6.6.
watch_item: Cliff detection and taxonomy may depend on model scale, sampling behavior, and benchmark distribution, so gains may not transfer uniformly to other tasks or decoding settings.
paper_title: Cliff Tokens: Identifying Single-Token Failure Triggers in LLM Mathematical Reasoning
